perturbation.original_text: 1. Agreement Term. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") will begin on the date hereof (the "Commencement Date"), and will continue until the earlier of (i) December 31, 2022, (ii) the termination or expiration of the Studio Lease (defined below), (iii) election to terminate and notice thereof given by Programmer to Licensee, and (iv) mutual written consent of Licensee and Programmer (the "Term"), unless extended or earlier terminated pursuant to Section 11 hereof. The term "Studio Lease" means that certain Lease dated as of February 23, 1996 of certain real estate located on the 7th Floor of an office building located at 395 Hudson St., New York, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Agreement Term. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") will begin on the date hereof (the "Commencement Date"), and will continue until December 31, 2023 (the "Term"), unless extended or earlier terminated pursuant to Section 11 hereof. The term "Studio Lease" means that certain Lease dated as of February 23, 1996 of certain real estate located on the 7th Floor of an office building located at 395 Hudson St., New York, New York.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the agreement terminates on the earliest of four conditions. The modified text changes the term to run until a fixed date (December 31, 2023) and removes the conditions of early termination dependent on the Studio Lease, the Programmer's election to terminate, and mutual written consent. This directly contradicts Section 11 which outlines termination events before the end of the original term, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the circumstances under which the contract may be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Events of Default; Termination. 11.1 Programmer's Events of Default. The occurrence of any of the following will be deemed an Event of Default by Programmer under this Agreement: (a) Programmer fails to observe or perform its obligations contained in this Agreement in any material respect; or (b) Programmer breaches the representations and warranties made by it under this Agreement in any material respect. 11.2 Licensee Events of Default. The occurrence of the following will be deemed an Event of Default by Licensee under this Agreement: (a) Licensee fails to observe or perform its obligations contained in this Agreement in any material respect; or (b) Licensee breaches the representations and warranties made by it under this Agreement in any material respect. 11.3 Cure Period. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any Event of Default will not be deemed to have occurred until fifteen (15) days after the non-defaulting party has provided the defaulting party with written notice specifying the Event of Default and such Event of Default remains uncured. - 3 - Source: EMMIS COMMUNICATIONS CORP, 8-K, 11/25/2019 11.4 Termination in the Event of Default. Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, and in the absence of a timely cure pursuant to Section 11.3, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement, effective immediately upon written notice to the defaulting party. 11.5 Cooperation Upon Termination. If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, the parties agree to cooperate with one another and to take all actions necessary to rescind this Agreement and return the parties to the status quo ante.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 1 to stipulate a fixed termination date (December 31, 2023), removing the original conditions for earlier termination, including those dependent on the Studio Lease, Programmer's election, and mutual consent. This directly contradicts Section 11, which outlines events of default and subsequent termination rights prior to the originally specified term. The removal of the earlier termination conditions creates a clear inconsistency with Section 11, making it unenforceable as the agreement would only terminate on December 31, 2023, regardless of any event of default outlined in Section 11. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term Payments. No payment is due from Programmer to Licensee for broadcast of the Programs pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term Payments. Programmer shall pay Licensee $10,000 per month for broadcast of the Programs pursuant to this Agreement, due on the 15th of each month, starting December 15, 2019. Failure to pay by the 30th of each month will result in a 10% late fee.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement explicitly states that no payments are due from the Programmer to the Licensee. The modified text introduces a payment obligation of $10,000 per month, which directly contradicts the original no-payment clause. This contradicts Section 11, which outlines Events of Default, potentially including failure to observe obligations under the agreement, but doesn't account for a scenario where payment terms are introduced mid-contract in a contradictory manner. Thus, this change creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about what constitutes a payment-related Event of Default.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Events of Default; Termination. 11.1 Programmer's Events of Default. The occurrence of any of the following will be deemed an Event of Default by Programmer under this Agreement: (a) Programmer fails to observe or perform its obligations contained in this Agreement in any material respect; or (b) Programmer breaches the representations and warranties made by it under this Agreement in any material respect. 11.2 Licensee Events of Default. The occurrence of the following will be deemed an Event of Default by Licensee under this Agreement: (a) Licensee fails to observe or perform its obligations contained in this Agreement in any material respect; or (b) Licensee breaches the representations and warranties made by it under this Agreement in any material respect. 11.3 Cure Period. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any Event of Default will not be deemed to have occurred until fifteen (15) days after the non-defaulting party has provided the defaulting party with written notice specifying the Event of Default and such Event of Default remains uncured. - 3 - Source: EMMIS COMMUNICATIONS CORP, 8-K, 11/25/2019 11.4 Termination in the Event of Default. Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, and in the absence of a timely cure pursuant to Section 11.3, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement, effective immediately upon written notice to the defaulting party. 11.5 Cooperation Upon Termination. If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, the parties agree to cooperate with one another and to take all actions necessary to rescind this Agreement and return the parties to the status quo ante.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'No payment is due from Programmer to Licensee'. The changed text introduces a payment obligation of $10,000 per month. This directly contradicts the 'no payment' clause. Section 11 outlines events of default, including failure to observe obligations. The introduction of a payment obligation that was previously not present, and the subsequent failure to pay it, directly contradicts the initial agreement, creating an event of default not previously contemplated in the original agreement and therefore creates a conflict within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Indemnification. Programmer shall indemnify and hold Licensee harmless against any and all liability arising from Programmer's use of Licensee's facilities, if any, or from the broadcast of the WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel, including without limitation for libel, slander, illegal competition or trade practice, infringement of trademarks, trade names, or program titles, violation of rights of privacy, and infringement of copyrights and proprietary rights or any other violation of third party rights or FCC rules or other applicable law. The obligations under this Section shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Indemnification. Licensee shall indemnify and hold Programmer harmless against any and all liability arising from Licensee's control over the Station, including the HD2 Channel, and any exercise of its rights under Section 6, including but not limited to rejecting or refusing programs which Licensee believes to be contrary to the public interest, or substituting programs which Licensee believes to be of greater local or national importance or which are designed to address the problems, needs and interests of the local communities. The obligations under this Section shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 12 states that the Programmer indemnifies the Licensee. The modified text reverses this, stating that the Licensee indemnifies the Programmer, specifically regarding the Licensee's control over the Station and its programming decisions. This creates a direct in-text contradiction concerning which party bears the responsibility for indemnification and under what circumstances, specifically contradicting the original intent of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Indemnification. Licensee shall indemnify and hold Programmer harmless against any and all liability arising from Licensee's control over the Station, including the HD2 Channel, and any exercise of its rights under Section 6, including but not limited to rejecting or refusing programs which Licensee believes to be contrary to the public interest, or substituting programs which Licensee believes to be of greater local or national importance or which are designed to address the problems, needs and interests of the local communities. The obligations under this Section shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Programmer indemnifies the Licensee, while the changed text reverses this, stating the Licensee indemnifies the Programmer. The contradicted text reiterates the Licensee indemnifying the Programmer. This is a direct in-text contradiction concerning which party bears the responsibility for indemnification, a key obligation, causing inconsistency as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
